threats.title: Weak Password Policy Leads to Account Takeover
threats.description: The system relies on username/password authentication, and if a weak password policy is in place (e.g., short passwords, lack of complexity requirements), attackers can easily guess or crack user credentials through brute-force or dictionary attacks. This allows unauthorized access to user accounts.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attack, credential stuffing, password cracking, phishing campaigns targeting users with weak passwords.
mitigations: Implement a strong password policy (minimum length, complexity requirements, regular password changes).
Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users.
Implement account lockout policies after multiple failed login attempts.

threats.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability in User Input Fields
threats.description: The application likely uses user-supplied input directly within SQL queries without proper sanitization or parameterization. Attackers can inject malicious SQL code to manipulate data, bypass authentication, or even execute arbitrary commands on the database server.
threats.attack_vector: Malicious input injected into forms, URL parameters, or API requests that are then executed as SQL queries.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Use an Object-Relational Mapper (ORM) that handles escaping and sanitization automatically.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious SQL injection attempts.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: The application could be vulnerable to denial-of-service attacks if it doesn't properly validate and sanitize user input. Attackers can submit large or specially crafted requests that consume excessive server resources, leading to performance degradation or complete system crashes.
threats.affected_component: Web Server, Application Server, Database Server
threats.attack_vector: Malformed requests, oversized file uploads, excessive API calls, or other input that overwhelms system resources.
mitigations: Implement input validation and sanitization to prevent malicious input.
Rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Implement caching mechanisms to reduce the load on the server.

threats.title: Exposure of Sensitive Data Due to Lack of Data Classification
threats.description: Without a defined data classification policy and enforcement mechanisms, sensitive data (e.g., personally identifiable information (PII), financial data) may be handled with inadequate security controls. This increases the risk of data breaches and unauthorized disclosure.
threats.affected_component: All Data Storage and Processing Components
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to databases, misconfigured servers, insecure APIs, or vulnerabilities in the application code.
mitigations: Implement a data classification policy defining data sensitivity levels.
Apply appropriate access controls based on data classification.
Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit.

threats.title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) Vulnerability
threats.description: If the application exposes internal object references (e.g., database keys, file names) directly in URLs or API requests without proper authorization checks, attackers can manipulate these references to access resources they shouldn't be able to access, potentially escalating their privileges.
threats.attack_vector: Direct manipulation of object references in URLs or API requests to access unauthorized data or functionality.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks to verify user permissions before accessing any internal object references.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) instead of direct identifiers.
Enforce the principle of least privilege – grant users only the minimum necessary access rights.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Password Policy Leads to Account Takeover
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: SQL Injection Vulnerability in User Input Fields
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposure of Sensitive Data Due to Lack of Data Classification
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
